skill.name: implementation-review
skill.description: Triggers plan and code review agents after planning or implementation work is complete.
skill.summary: Enforces review checkpoints for planning and implementation workflows.
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found Markdown formatting and descriptive front matter only. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts were found in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick matches are Markdown inline code, a Markdown table, or fenced examples describing agent names and workflow text. No shell command execution, script body, or user-controlled command construction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation, and the cited lines contain inline code spans, tables, or fenced example text. There is no interpreter context or command execution API.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: Line 3 is YAML front matter that describes trigger phrases for review automation. No hash function, encryption function, key handling, or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain description text and contains no code. The weak cryptography label is not supported by the file content.

content.value_statement: Planning and coding workflows can move forward without a required quality check. This skill routes completed plans and implementations to review agents before the next phase begins.
actual_capabilities: Detects planning completion phrases and routes plans to a plan review agent.
Detects implementation completion phrases and routes changed code to a code review agent.
Defines review focus areas for feasibility, risk, maintainability, security, and performance.
Requires blocked review results to stop progression to the next workflow phase.
Provides a structured review report format for findings and action items.
limitations: Requires the named review agents to exist in the host environment.
Does not implement review agent logic by itself.
Depends on completion signals or workflow conventions being followed.
Does not modify code during review.

use_cases.title: Gate Implementation Plans
use_cases.description: Send completed implementation strategies to a plan reviewer before coding starts.

use_cases.title: Review Finished Code
use_cases.description: Trigger a code reviewer when an implementation task reaches the ready for review stage.

use_cases.title: Standardize Review Reports
use_cases.description: Use one report structure for approvals, revisions, blocked items, and required actions.

prompt_templates.title: Request a Plan Review
prompt_templates.prompt: The strategic-planner has completed the implementation strategy. Use implementation-review to send the plan for review.

prompt_templates.title: Request a Code Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Implementation is complete and the changed files are ready. Use implementation-review to trigger the code review step.

prompt_templates.title: Handle a Blocked Review
prompt_templates.prompt: The review status is BLOCKED. Use implementation-review constraints to identify required revisions before the workflow continues.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an EPIC Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this EPIC workflow and confirm whether plan review and code review gates are applied at the correct phases.

output_examples.input: The strategic-planner has completed the implementation strategy for T001.
output_examples.output: The plan is routed to the plan consultant for completeness, feasibility, risk, and alignment review.

output_examples.input: Implementation is done and the feature is ready for review.
output_examples.output: The changed files are routed to the code reviewer for quality, security, performance, and standards review.
The workflow stops before commit and reports the required action items for revision.

best_practices: Use clear completion phrases so the review trigger is easy to detect.
Attach the relevant plan or changed files before requesting review.
Resolve blocked findings before moving to the next workflow phase.
anti_patterns: Skipping review because the implementation appears small.
Continuing the workflow after a blocked review status.
Expecting this skill to replace the actual plan or code reviewer agents.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It does not review content directly. It routes plans and code to the appropriate review agents.

faq.question: Which agents does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects a plan-consultant agent for plans and a code-reviewer agent for implementation work.

faq.question: Can it modify code after review?
faq.answer: No. The skill says reviews should report findings and should not modify code.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when a plan is complete or when implementation work is ready for review.

faq.question: Does it enforce blocked status?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the workflow to stop if a review status is blocked.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
